programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: The wanted criminal Richard Bruno Riddick arrives on a planet called Helion Prime and finds himself up against an invading empire called the Necromongers, an army that plans to convert or kill all humans in the universe.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed vacation to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: There is a new criminal mastermind at large (Professor Moriarty) and not only is he Holmes’ intellectual equal, but his capacity for evil and lack of conscience may give him an advantage over the detective.

programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: When a virus leaks from a top-secret facility, turning all resident researchers into ravenous zombies and their lab animals into mutated hounds from hell, the government sends in an elite military task force to contain the outbreak.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: Every Which Way But Loose
programme.desc: Philo Beddoe is your regular, easygoing, truck-driving guy. He's also the best bar-room brawler west of the Rockies. And he lives with a 165-pound orangutan named Clyde. Like other guys, Philo finally falls in love - with a flighty singer who leads him on a screwball chase across the American Southwest. Nothing's in the way except a motorcycle gang, some cops, and legendary brawler Tank Murdock.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: In a future where a failed global-warming experiment kills off most life on the planet, a class system evolves aboard the Snowpiercer; a train that travels around the globe via a perpetual-motion engine.

programme.title: Predator
programme.desc: A team of commandos on a mission in a Central American jungle find themselves hunted by an extraterrestrial warrior.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
programme.desc: In this third installment of the adrenaline-fueled action franchise, skilled assassin John Wick (Keanu Reeves) returns with a $14 million price tag on his head and an army of bounty-hunting killers on his trail. After killing a member of the shadowy international assassin's guild, the High Table, John Wick is excommunicado, but the world's most ruthless hit men and women await his every turn.

programme.title: Pitch Black
programme.desc: A transport ship crashes and leaves its crew stranded on a desert planet inhabited by bloodthirsty creatures that come out during an eclipse.

programme.title: The Mummy's Curse
programme.desc: An irrigation project in the rural bayous of Louisiana unearths living mummy Kharis, who was buried in quicksand twenty-five years earlier.

programme.title: The Mummy's Ghost
programme.desc: An Egyptian high priest travels to America to reclaim the bodies of ancient Egyptian princess Ananka and her living guardian mummy Kharis. Learning that Ananka's spirit has been reincarnated into another body, he kidnaps a young woman of Egyptian descent with a mysterious resemblance to the princess. However, the high priest's greedy desires cause him to lose control of the mummy.

programme.title: The Mummy's Hand
programme.desc: A couple of comical, out-of-work archaeologists (Dick Foran and Wallace Ford) in Egypt discover evidence of the burial place of the ancient Egyptian princess Ananka. After receiving funding from an eccentric magician (Cecil Kellaway) and his beautiful daughter (Peggy Moran), they set out into the desert only to be terrorized by a sinister high priest (George Zucco) and the living mummy Kharis (Tom Tyler) who are the guardians of Ananka^Òs tomb.

programme.title: The Mummy's Tomb
programme.desc: A high priest travels to America with a living mummy to kill those who had desecrated the tomb of an Egyptian princess thirty years earlier.

programme.title: The People Under the Stairs
programme.desc: When Fool breaks into the home of his family's landlords, he discovers that they have mutilated several boys and kept them imprisoned under stairs. As Fool attempts to flee, he meets their daughter, Alice. Can Fool and Alice escape?

programme.title: The Toxic Avenger
programme.desc: When a downtrodden janitor, Winston Gooze, is exposed to a catastrophic toxic accident, he’s transformed into a new kind of hero: The Toxic Avenger. Now, Toxie must rise from outcast to savior, taking on ruthless corporate overlords and corrupt forces who threaten his son, his friends, and his community.

programme.title: The Wolf Man
programme.desc: Upon his return to his father's estate, aristocrat Larry Talbot meets a beautiful woman, attends a mystical carnival and uncovers a horrifying curse.

programme.title: They Live
programme.desc: Nada, a down-on-his-luck construction worker, discovers a pair of special sunglasses. Wearing them, he is able to see the world as it really is: people being bombarded by media and government with messages like "Stay Asleep," "No Imagination," "Submit to Authority." Even scarier is that he is able to see that some usually normal-looking people are in fact ugly aliens in charge of the massive campaign to keep humans subdued.

programme.title: Thir13en Ghosts
programme.desc: When Cyrus Kriticos, a very rich collector of unique things, dies, he leaves his house, fortune, and his prized collection of ghosts.

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A small town gradually becomes aware of a strange creature which picks off people one by one. But what is this creature, and where is it? At the same time, a seismologist is working in the area, she detects _tremors_. The creature lives underground, and can 'pop up' without warning. Trapped in their town, the town-folk have no escape.

programme.title: Tremors II: Aftershocks
programme.desc: Earl Bassett, now a washed-up ex-celebrity, is hired by a Mexican oil company to eradicate a Graboid epidemic that's killing more people each day. However, the humans aren't the only one with a new battle plan.

programme.title: Tremors 3: Back to Perfection
programme.desc: The now famous Burt Gummer returns to his hometown of Perfection, Nevada for the first time in years, but the deadliest Graboid evolution yet forces him to save the town he swore to protect.

programme.title: Werewolf of London
programme.desc: While on a botanical expedition in Tibet Dr. Wilfred Glendon is attacked in the dark by a strange animal. Returning to London, he finds himself turning nightly into a werewolf and terrorizing the city, with the only hope for curing his affliction a rare Asian flower, the Mariphasa.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: Seeking shelter from a storm, five travelers are in for a bizarre and terrifying night when they stumble upon the Femm family estate.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: ACDC - Live at Caste Donington, England (August 17, 1991)
The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
World Championship Wrestling - 3x01 - World Championship Wrestling 87
World Championship Wrestling - 3x02 - World Championship Wrestling 88
World Championship Wrestling - 3x03 - World Championship Wrestling 89
World Championship Wrestling - 3x04 - World Championship Wrestling 90
World Championship Wrestling - 3x05 - World Championship Wrestling 91
World Championship Wrestling - 3x06 - World Championship Wrestling 92
World Championship Wrestling - 3x07 - World Championship Wrestling 93
World Championship Wrestling - 3x08 - World Championship Wrestling 94
World Championship Wrestling - 3x09 - World Championship Wrestling 95
World Championship Wrestling - 3x10 - World Championship Wrestling 96
World Championship Wrestling - 3x11 - World Championship Wrestling 97
World Championship Wrestling - 3x12 - World Championship Wrestling 98
World Championship Wrestling - 3x13 - World Championship Wrestling 99
World Championship Wrestling - 3x14 - World Championship Wrestling 100
World Championship Wrestling - 3x15 - World Championship Wrestling 101
World Championship Wrestling - 3x16 - World Championship Wrestling 102
World Championship Wrestling - 3x18 - World Championship Wrestling 103
World Championship Wrestling - 3x19 - World Championship Wrestling 104
World Championship Wrestling - 3x20 - World Championship Wrestling 105
World Championship Wrestling - 3x21 - World Championship Wrestling 106
World Championship Wrestling - 3x22 - World Championship Wrestling 107
World Championship Wrestling - 3x23 - World Championship Wrestling 108
The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges run a furniture store and come into possession of a stolen pearl necklace. Three crooked dames convince the boys that the necklace is theirs, and when the real thieves arrive, the stooges fight to defend the girl's property.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne talks Leon into letting her play Santa Claus at the mall -- and hears something she wishes she hadn't from one of the kid's mothers, who turns out to be Darlene's new friend and confidante.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: It is Crystal's choice to choose where the gals' night out will be. Crystal chooses bingo and they all hate it. After Roseanne wins $50 playing bingo, she gets hooked on it. Becky and Darlene babysit Little Ed, but when Dan comes home its D.J. and Dan that end up taking care of the baby. Roseanne and Crystal get into a fight, but at the end they make up and Roseanne learns that Crystal is pregnant again.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: When Dan and Arnie struggle to bring their bowling team out of last place, some unexpected competition arises. Meanwhile, Roseanne feels shut out of Jackie's new friendship with Nancy.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: After Roseanne throws her back out, Bev arrives to help out -- and to spoil her grandchildren.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: A doctor recommends that Roseanne undergo breast reduction surgery for her back problems.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: Breakin' Up is Hard to Do
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan breathe a sigh of relief when Becky breaks up with Mark.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: On a last visit to their childhood home, Roseanne and Jackie recall their adolescent years, causing Roseanne to reconsider a punishment for Darlene, who skipped school with David to attend a comic book convention in Chicago.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The Conners are sitting pretty for a TV ad for Rodbell's Luncheonette, but at home things get ugly between D.J. and Todd. Leon and Steven break up, and the Bowmans move back to Chicago.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr.Grace will soon be 80 years old and Mr.Rumbold has designated the staff to come up with an idea for a special birthday celebration. They decide on spoofing "This is Your Life" by doing a presentation of "Here is Your Department," showing how Mr.Grace has affected all of their lives.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Captain Peacock doesn't have his usual carnation and is in a very bad mood, but now that Mr. Grace has returned from America, carnations will no longer be needed, along with a lot of other things. In fact the entire manner of the floor will be changing. Casual dress and first-name terms within the department are only the beginning!

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill is sponsored by Cleam and gets into an epic car race!
Featured cartoons: Gee Whiz-z-z-z-z-z-z (1956) Fool Coverage (1952) Mutts About Racing (1958) Greedy for Tweety (1957) The Hasty Hare (1952)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: Riley Greenleaf is a publisher and Alan Mallory, a successful writer, is his principal asset. But Alan Mallory has decided to change publishers. Riley threatens to kill Alan. Then he hires Eddie Kane, a Vietnam veteran, to murder Alan and to leave evidence that the killer is Riley himself, who has an excellent alibi. But a key will betray him.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Mrs Naugatuck has a party but does not invite Maude & Walter. When Maude accidentally breaks Mrs Naugatuck's urn, Mrs Naugatuck thinks it is revenge for not being invited to the party. Then Mrs Naugatuck purposely breaks an item of Maude's.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: While riding home on the subway one evening, Maude strikes up a friendship with a young West Indian woman. However, Maude discovers her wallet is missing after she leaves. Maude thinks the woman did it. The jokes on her when she discovers the woman is a new maid.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose has been dating Dr. Jonathan Newman and begins to think their relationship is leading to the altar. However she begins to have second thoughts especially since he's a midget.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy latest boyfriend Glen gives her a shock when he tells her that he's married. She surprises herself when she finds herself sneaking around to be with him. Meanwhile, Blanche unloads her old car on Rose in order to buy a new one.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: After Rose's boyfriend Al dies of a heart attack in her bed, Rose fears that she is a death jinx in the bedroom because her beloved husband Charlie died of a heart attack in her bed as well. To make matters worse, after Rose tracks down Al's sister to notify her of his death, she comes to find that his "sister" is really in fact his wife!

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: It is revealed that Blanche has another brother - a mentally handicapped man who has been living in an institution.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred is sure that his dreams will be good luck in picking numbers for a lottery and his hunch turns out to be true, despite the fact that Lamont forbids him to participate. Fred quickly finds himself $500 richer but also finds Aunt Ethel and Bubba needing quick cash.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: After getting in an accident with the truck, Fred thinks that he might have whiplash and Fred's friend Bubba convinces him that he can make big money off of the situation. Lamont is against the whole idea but Fred insists on going through with a lawsuit, but first Fred must identify the person the hit him.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike becomes the sole support of a growing family when Gloria loses her job because of her pregnancy.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie feels abandoned after Edith begins to volunteer part-time at the Sunshine Home for the Elderly.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie's company physical is fast approaching, and he needs to remain calm for an entire weekend for the blood pressure test. Of course, a variety of situations arise that test Archie's ability to keep an even temper.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Ben & Gary vs. Marlana & Raquel vs. Lori & Val
programme.desc: Lori & Val would later appear in the Sweep of Champions

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Laurie & Buddy vs. Teesha & Dea vs. Neef & Sooze

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges stencil all the wrong names on the office doors when they are janitors, which causes a rich old lady to think that Moe is really a famous decorator. She hires them to come redo her house, which ends disastrous, especially once Curly goes crazy from seeing tassels.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in the stone age, the stooges are cavemen who must have various misadventures hunting, gathering, and otherwise coping with prehistoric life. When some other cavemen threaten to take their women ("Aggie", "Maggie", and "Baggy"), the boys fight them off with a catapulting tree branch that shoots rocks and eggs.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in the old west, the stooges are three tramps wanted for vagrancy. After ruining a medicine peddlers show, they arrive in Peaceful Gulch where a picture has been printed declaring them to be three famous lawmen coming to clean up the town. Assigned to guard the bank, the boys have the local gang scared at first, but when the gang learns who the stooges really are, they rob the bank. The boys go in pursuit, find the bad guy's hideout, subdue the bandits and recover the money

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are actors who can't seem to find a job, so they decide to jump off a high building and end it all. On the roof top they meet three girl dancers with the same idea. Before they can jump, they meet a millionaire Broadway producer who hires them all for his next show. The rehearsal goes so well that he doubles their salary, but it all comes to naught when they discover that the "producer" is an escaped patient from Dr. Dippy's retreat.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Rejected by the armed services, the stooges decide to "do their bit" by becoming farmers. After paying $1000 and throwing in their car, the boys are owners of a run down farm, which lacks any livestock. After capturing an escaped ostrich, they decide to carve jack-o- lanterns for profit and then must contend with some Japanese who have escaped from a relocation center. The stooges become heroes by capturing the escapees, with the help of explosive eggs laid by the ostrich who had swallowed some blasting powder.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are hired as reporters and their first assignment is to get a picture of a visiting prince who is planning to marry a local socialite. The boys disguise as servants and infiltrate a party being in thrown in the honor of the prince. The stooges ruin the party, but save the day as they expose the prince as crook who is planning to rob the house. Their boss is so grateful for the expose that he gives the boys a bonus and the rich lady decides to marry Curly!

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Three bumbling waiters try to help a clinically depressed little girl by locating her kidnapped father. The Stooges operate a restaurant and try to help a little girl who becomes ill when her father, a bail bondsman, disappears with a lot of cash and the Stooges have to fight gangsters.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Bart commits an extremely destructive prank, he is given Focusyn, a pill to help him concentrate in school. At first the results are impressive, and then Bart starts believing in a conspiracy involving spy satellites and major league baseball.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Guess Who's Coming to Criticize Dinner?
programme.desc: With Lisa's help, Homer uses his vast knowledge of food to become the town's leading restaurant critic. But when he gets petty and vindictive in his criticisms, all the other chefs in town try to kill him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In "I Know What You Diddily-Iddly-Did" the Simpsons fear they have killed Ned Flanders. "Desperately Xeeking Xena" satirizes superheroes of yore with "Stretch Dude" Bart and "Clobber Girl" Lisa. And "Life's A Glitch, Then You Die" depicts the upcoming horror of Y2K.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al become the neighborhood stud when some women see him move a couch.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Kelly moves out after Al beats up yet another of her boyfriends. This upsets Peggy, who becomes an overprotective mom, to Bud's dislike.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After Al's bachelor uncle dies, he leaves $500,000 to the first of his relatives to produce a newborn baby named after him. Al overcomes his usual aversion to sex with Peg. Unknown to him, she is secretly staying on the pill, to keep Al wanting sex with her, and having decided that no amount of money is worth going through pregnancy a third time.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After a drinking binge at a bankers party, Marcy discovers herself to be married to a mysterious hunk named Jefferson D'Arcy, whom Al unearths as a gigolo and con artist. To make Marcy feel better about this unexpected change in her life, Peggy decides to throw a formal wedding ceremony for Marcy and Jefferson in the Bundy's back yard, a ceremony in which nothing goes as planned.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al becomes the neighborhood godfather when Kelly starts dating a 41-year-old man with the right connections.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Told from the point of view of Buck, he runs away from the house feeling neglected. Behind a diner, he picks up a female dog, whom he brings home. He quickly regrets it, as the new dog gets all the attention from Bud and Kelly. Meanwhile, Al is obsessed with getting a taste of his favorite cherry cheesecake from a restaurant in Wisconsin.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After spending $2,000 by going to decorating school, Peggy defies Al degree not to redecorate by turning his bathroom sanctuary in the garage into a chamber of "filthy, feminine horror." Al passes out when he sees his mighty Ferguson toilet adorned with Peggy's trappings.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When a second floor aerobics studio, full of terribly overweight women, collapses into the shoe store, Al is laid off while the store is being repaired. Al then gets a night job as a security guard at his old high school, Polk High. This prompts his former high school rival, Spare Tire Dixon, to steal his prized trophy which leads to a brutal showdown.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Julio has moved out of his house and Fred and Lamont decide to buy it and turn it into a boarding house and call it The Sanford Arms. To get enough money for the project, Fred and Lamont go down to the bank to apply for a loan but soon find themselves hostages in a bank robbery attempt.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Julio's house has been transformed into Fred and Lamont's boarding house called The Sanford Arms. Now they've come to the task of finding some boarders and soon find themselves bombarded with an endless supply of crazies, including a hooker, an ex-con, a hippie, a newlywed couple, and Hoppy's eccentric mother.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred and Lamont are appalled when they see a sitcom, Steinberg and Son which amazing seems to be modeled on the life of Fred and Lamont. Fred and Lamont decide to check it out themselves and go to a taping of an episode and are shocked to see the similarites. In fact, the only thing different from the sitcom and Fred and Lamont's lives are the names of the characters and the fact that the cast is white. Lamont and Fred hire a lawyer and prepare a million dollar lawsuit againt the producers.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: Brother, Can You Spare an Act?
programme.desc: Fred's sister, Frances and her white husband, Rodney have been having some hard times and they stop by to ask Fred if they could stay with him for awhile. Meanwhile, problems at the Sanford Arms has Fred considering giving Rodney a job as general manager. But Rodney may not accept it since he's scheduled to perform in an act at a nightclub.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris turns 31 and decides it's time to move out, so he goes in search of a new place to live and he moves into Gus' garage.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: After listening to Chris remind him of what a living hell his life truly is, Larry runs away, so Chris goes in search of him and thinks he found him--but it's actually just a hobo who speaks no English.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.sub-title: You Ought to Be in Pictures
programme.desc: Daffy Duck convinces Porky Pig to quit the cartoon biz and try his luck in the features. Porky's adventures begin when he tries to enter the studio. Features live-action segments with producer Leon Schlesinger as himself and studio writer Michael Maltese (uncredited, with his voice dubbed by Mel Blanc) as a studio guard. Studio directors Bob Clampett and Chuck Jones also have brief cameos.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A collection of short gags based on classic nursery rhymes and fairy tales.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Spot-gag parody taking place at the circus.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: The first "true" appearance of Bugs Bunny and Elmer Fudd. While hunting rabbits, Elmer Fudd comes across Bugs Bunny who tricks and harasses the hunter.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A junior ghost finds an advertisement to scare ghosts out of a haunted house.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Spot-gag of airplanes flying over mid-west America.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Spot-gag about all the holidays of the year.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head try to mail themselves to Spring Break.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Middle-aged Beavis and Butt-Head pose as vets for free beer.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Buzzcut orders Beavis and Butt-head to buy athletic supporters for gym class; Daria takes a revealing picture of them for the school newspaper.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head mistake their neighborhood polling place for a strip club.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head find an ATM card, get some cash with it and get hunted by the law on TV.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: No license, no money, no problem! Beavis and Butt-Head shanghai a used car salesman on a joyride that leaves the car in pieces. Will they walk away from this one, or is everything wrecked for good?

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Middle-aged Beavis and Butt-Head get a warehouse job at the world's biggest internet retail company. You know the one we mean.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to a lingerie store and feel up the merchandise.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head try to harvest honey from a wasps' nest.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: The ants are loose, the ants are loose! While Pee Wee is playing house with Miss Yvonne and the Playhouse Gang, the ants sneak out of their farm, go marching into the Dinosaur Family's home, steal a cake and get all over everybody and everything. "What" will Pee Wee do? The 'Ants In Your Pants Dance!

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: A giant one-eyed monster is on the loose and appears in the Playhouse, scaring everyone! But after Pee-wee makes the monster a snack and Jambi grants Pee-wee's wish to understand the monster, the whole gang has a new friend! They even play a game of golf.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline figure out a surprising fundraising solution when a potential storefront for Max's Homemade Cupcake becomes available for the girls to rent as the two go to great lengths to raise enough money to see their dream come true.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline decide to hire an intern to help take care of some of the menial tasks involved with the preparation for opening their new cupcake shop, but Max seems conflicted with her new managerial role as the boss

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: As the road to WrestleMania V continues, Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan present an exciting lineup of in-ring competition on this edition of Prime Time Wrestling, featuring a memorable singles bout between Bret "Hit Man" Hart and "The Million Dollar Man" Ted DiBiase. Plus, Jake "The Snake" Roberts, King Haku, and more in action.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: The Belchers beg Bob to buy a food truck to compete against the ones that are stealing his customers. When the "Lolla-Pa-Foods-A-Festival" comes up, the Belchers bring the truck and the kids decide to cause mischief. Unfortunately, this causes a riot to rise from the other food truckers.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra is upset when she sees Ray's giddy reaction to her friend's recently acquired "new and improved" breasts. Anxious to find out whether or not Ray is happy with her body, Debra decides to conduct a little experiment and makes some minor enhancement to her upper region.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: To fix his own dinner, Ray tries to open a tin of tuna with a new can opener Debra has bought. He spills tuna water and blames the can opener. This incident is shared by Debra and Ray with Robert and Marie respectively, but their viewpoints are radically different.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie recall the first time they met.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Arthur's 75th birthday is coming up and Doug and Carrie decide to throw him a surprise party, but they're in for a rude awakening when two of the invited guests are at each others throats and they can't find Arthur!

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine house-sits for Mr. Pitt. Jerry goes to meet up with her to go to the movies and winds up offending the Doorman. So to make up for it, Jerry watches the door. But when Jerry leaves his post and the couch in the lobby is stolen, Jerry and Elaine formulate a plan to cover their tracks. Meanwhile, Kramer invents a bra for men and George tries to find a way to get his father out of his apartment.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: A gym member teams with George in business and makes a date with Elaine to see Mel Torme; Jerry suspects his dentist of hanky-panky.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky volunteers to take over cooking duties for the Conners' Thanksgiving; Dan and Mark are still at each other's throats; Fred is shocked at the dysfunction of the family he might marry into; and Nana Mary reveals a long-kept secret about Bev's marriage.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Leon convinces Jackie and Nancy to open the restaurant for breakfast, causing Roseanne to fear he is planning to force her out. Roseanne's car is stolen -- by D.J., and her violent reaction frightens her that she may repeat the pattern of her own abuse as a child, while Dan blames her anger on work stress.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Pam is sporting a new look at work with her glasses. She admits she spent the night at a "friend's" house and forgot her contact solution. Michael makes sure to point out that the glasses make her look ugly, and if she wants to be hot, the glasses need to come off.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Jim is wearing his best golfing uniform because he is hitting the links with a potential client, Phil Maguire. This is a very important account for Jim because he needs to look good to Ryan, who recently put him on probation for his job performance. Andy and Kevin are also joining in the game to show their support.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Fleeing from a duel, Homer takes his family to live on a farm. There he discovers that by mixing tomatoes, tobacco and radioactivity from the nuclear plant, he can create a dangerously delicious new substance.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer briefly becomes famous after bowling a perfect 300 at the local ten-pin alley. However,his celebrity quickly wanes so he decides to bond with Maggie instead. He tries to teach her to swim but she ends up rescuing him after he gets into difficulties. As a reward he takes her bowling where she also gets the perfect score.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Due to overuse of fertility medication, Apu and wife Manjula give birth to octuplets. Desperate for money, Apu loans the babies to an unscrupulous carny promoter, then needs Homer's help to get them back.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In a scheme to get Krusty's autograph, Bart sends him a check. When Krusty's offshore holding corporation endorses the check, his illegal tax shelter is exposed. In despair after the government auctions off his possessions, Krusty fakes his own death.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: While researching into Jebediah Springfield's past for an essay, Lisa discovers that the town founder wasn't the hero that everyone thinks he was.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Burns's personal assistant Smithers goes on holiday and Homer is chosen to be his stand-in. However he is so useless that Burns learns to cope for himself, making Smithers redundant when he returns to Springfield. Smithers and Homer get into a fight, during which Burns is thrown through a window. This means that he will now be very reliant on Smithers after all.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: "Stone Cold" Steve Austin and The Undertaker defend the Tag Team Championship against The Rock & Owen Hart. A rift in DX begins to form when X-Pac and Triple H go one-on-one for a chance at the Intercontinental Championship. Mr. Yamaguchi-San and Kaentai seek revenge on Val Venis. Plus, the New Age Outlaws in action and the Brawl for All rolls on.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny reluctantly agrees to speak at the funeral of a local man he doesn’t really know. Alexis struggles with her new boyfriend’s love of animals.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira attend Roland’s annual Hawaiian bash. Alexis and Ted throw a couple’s dinner party, while David and Stevie’s relationship heats up.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Han fires Caroline when she insults his diner, so Max tries to mend their relationship.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline is in a tizzy when she develops a rash after a romantic evening with her former boyfriend.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are invited to be extras on a popular TV show shooting a scene in Han’s diner, but when Caroline catches the director’s eye, he offers her an actual role – with strings attached.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline’s father asks her to go on a talk show to defend him when a former employee threatens to defame him in a tell-all book.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: As Caroline and Max deep-clean part of the diner for Han before a health inspection, they make a discovery that could help relaunch their cupcake business; Sophie and Oleg break up after he cheats on her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Word about a shocking incident involving a celebrity brings customers to Max and Caroline's new cupcake window.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline tries to raise money for an expensive pair of new pants through an online fundraising site. Meanwhile, Max’s new smartphone comes between her and Caroline.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Kitty Kitty Spank Spank
programme.desc: When Max becomes smitten with an old stray cat that's been hanging around the apartment, Caroline tries to convince her they need to find it a better home. But just when they think they've found one, the cat's fate takes a turn.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline can’t figure out how to use the new cappuccino maker they bought for their cupcake business, they take temporary side jobs as baristas at a coffee house to learn the ropes.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline start losing business to the new cronut craze (half-croissant, half-donut), they come up with their own trend – cake fries.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Feeling guilty for buying hair extensions with their joint funds, Caroline splurges on a new bedding set for Max but gives the old one to charity, not realizing her old pillowcase has sentimental value.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After Han emails photos of a stripper’s face to his mother claiming she’s his girlfriend, he begs Max and Caroline to find her and convince her to play his girlfriend when his mother arrives from Korea.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline’s date is a no-show after promising to take her to one of the city’s hippest new restaurants, she and Max decide to take advantage of the hard-to-get reservation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The girls get a cleaning woman to clean the apartment and the woman unearths an application Max has for pastry school. Caroline urges to go and apply but Max doesn't think she can get in. They go and Caroline finds herself attracted to the instructor Nicolas. He asks Max to make a tart as her project to see if she can get in. Max thinks it's a piece of cake till she finds out what a tart is.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The First Day Of School
programme.desc: Max attempts to make a good impression on her first day of pastry school, but finds the class clown to be extremely distracting – and intriguing. Meanwhile, Caroline dresses to impress Max’s handsome teacher, Nicolas.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline attends her former nanny’s funeral, thinking the family will be honored to have her, but quickly finds out they have no idea who she is.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: After a tragic football accident devastates a normally bubbly Dani Rojas, AFC Richmond brings in a sports psychologist. Meanwhile the team battles a seemingly unending tie-streak and Keeley tries to point a newly retired Roy in a new direction.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Ted is surprised by the reappearance of a familiar face. Roy tries out a new gig.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Rebecca has a special visitor shadow her at work. A player's return is not welcomed by the team.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: At therapy, Tony likens himself to the 'sad clown,' but Melfi doesn't buy into his melancholy portrait. Meanwhile, Carmela drags A.J. with her to Furio's house to offer decorating tips and companionship; Paulie puts the finishing touches on some free artwork; and Christopher finds himself at a crossroads.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: After a sitdown with Carmine and Johnny Sack, Tony contemplates the future of the HUD partnership. Meanwhile, Paulie blows off steam with an old pal; Janice starts losing patience with the obsessive Bobby; and Melfi ponders the Freudian undercurrents of Tony's latest dreams.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael approaches things from a new angle. Eleanor, Chidi, Tahani and Jason struggle to make a collective decision.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Tahani throws a dinner party to impress. Eleanor and Jason both have to lend a hand when things don't go as planned.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Chidi and Eleanor tackle a famous ethical dilemma, leading to a conflict with Michael. Meanwhile, Tahani harbors a secret and confides in Janet.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: With the Greens in King's Landing having seated Aegon II on the Iron Throne, Rhaenyra and Daemon count their dragons and prepare for war. Lucerys is sent on a diplomatic mission to Storm's End with Aemond seeking retribution as the course of Targaryen history is changed forever.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Jon Arryn, the Hand of the King, is dead. King Robert Baratheon plans to ask his oldest friend, Eddard Stark, to take Jon's place. Across the sea, Viserys Targaryen plans to wed his sister to a nomadic warlord in exchange for an army.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: While Bran recovers from his fall, Ned takes only his daughters to Kings Landing. Jon Snow goes with his uncle Benjen to The Wall. Tyrion joins them.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House decides to awaken a comatose patient so he can question the man regarding the family history of his son, who may have a genetic condition and the father is the only living relative. Meanwhile, Wilson confronts House about the stolen prescription pad as Tritter approaches Cameron, Chase, and Foreman in an attempt to divide the team and reveal their loyalties.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: An 18-year-old teenager is brought to the hospital after having a heart attack. House reviews the boy's file and believes he has the diagnosis. He then turns the case into a game by sealing his opinion in an envelope and challenging Cameron, Foreman, and Chase to guess House's diagnosis on their own. Meanwhile, Tritter's actions against Wilson continue to strain the oncologist's relationship with House and destroy his ability to practice medicine.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart meets the original animator who created Itchy, penniless some 70 years after the studio stole his idea. When Bart helps him sue for restitution, he unwittingly bankrupts Itchy & Scratchy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Washed-up movie star Troy McClure is pulled over for driving without corrective lenses, and forced to go to the DMV to get his eyes checked. While there, he meets Selma, who he asks out for dinner. Spotted by a reporter, he quickly realizes that being seen in public with a woman will grab enough headlines to revitalize his career, leading to a whirlwind romance between him and Selma.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart creates a fake driver's license for himself and uses it to go on a spring break road trip with Milhouse, Nelson, and Martin. Meanwhile, Homer and Lisa bond after spending some quality time together at work.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Milhouse wonder if anything interesting happens to the citizens of Springfield, which leads to a series of interconnected vignettes.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Raging Abe Simpson and His Grumbling Grandson in 'The Curse of the Flying Hellfish'
programme.desc: When a fellow veteran dies, Grandpa Simpson and Mr. Burns fight for buried WWII treasure as the only surviving members of their battalion.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Mayor Quimby uses immigrants as a political scapegoat, the Simpsons discover that Apu is an illegal immigrant.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: To show his kids how hip he is, Homer takes Bart and Lisa to a music festival, where he earns himself a place in the sideshow by catching a cannonball with his belly.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The end of the school year, and an empty yearbook causes Lisa to contemplate her lack of popularity. When Ned Flanders is summoned for jury duty and offers Homer a chance to stay at his beach house, Lisa sees this as the opportunity to reinvent herself as a slacker in the hope of gaining friends, which proves to be far more successful than she could possibly imagine --- but not if Bart has any say in the matter.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Michael Jackson Gets Old and Wrinkled will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross drags Vanessa back onto the show 3 years after she left because she still has an episode under her contract (because the Adoption episode was banned). Meanwhile, Chris is nervous about being fired because of his upcoming birthday and fear that he has reached ""the age"".

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Fat Boys Eat New York will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The show deals with the problems of excess: too much slime, too many cast members, too much wardrobe, etc...

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Pee-Wee Herman Wins the Mr. Macho America Contest will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast is willing to do anything to become cool.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Superman Loses His Tights will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Nick enlists the help of the Genie of the Lost Sock to find Chris' jacket and a camera the Nick borrowed from Ross. In the search, he finds everything that every cast member of the show since it first went on the air had lost.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: Ross doesn't show up for work, leaving the kids to run this week's show.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Wright Brothers Lost Their Luggage will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross tries to improve the show's ratings by having this episode be the first to be shot in several different locations.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Parents and Teachers Celebrate the Cancellation of You Can't Do That On Television will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross decides to show how all of the countries around the world celebrate special events.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Rambo Thinks will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Some of the cast members try to do certain things, all unsuccessfully. Nick tries to remember his name, Sariya tries to keep her new hairstyle from getting slimed, Christian tries to nice to Jennifer, and Jennifer tries to wear a dress.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Roseanne Barr Goes Synchronized Swimming will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross goes sports crazy. He makes Chris use the eternal flame to light the gas grill, causing him to blow up, he uses special effects to give Carlos athletes foot, and he uses a ""Test Your Strength"" machine to scam the cast out of money.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The footlockers take on the game boys (and girls) in this first episode of 2005. Kenny and Vic also try out some new athletic footwear.
Games include: Spare Me; Sinkers and Floaters; Chum in the Mouth; Boulder Dash

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
It's the septic sluggers vs. TV pluggers as waste management takes on advertising. Vic and Kenny discuss the significant role of advertising in American culture by exploring the ad campaign for Cocoa Puffs.
Games include: Bagel Bumpers; Sperm Wheelers; Tumbling Dominoes of Doom; Rotating Surfboard of Death

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Only MXC can bring together bakers and malcontents for an all-out war.
Games include: Bust-a-Nut; Bird Droppings; Irritable Bowl Syndrome; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Mall Workers vs. The Telephone Company
programme.desc:
MXC creates a war between those who work at the mall and the telephone industry. The Captain revels in memories of better days spent at the mall and Guy crank calls employees who work the emergency line.
Games include: Eat Shitake; Circle Jerkers; In and Outhouse; Dope on a Rope

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
It is the battle of the still life vs. whole life as the art world takes on the insurance industry. Kenny tries to break into the art world and Vic tries to get Kenny added to his life insurance policy.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Dead End Zone; Hand Job; Wall Buggers

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The high societers take on the fishermen in this episode of MXC. Guy, meanwhile, relives lonely days spent on the LeDouche compound on Lake Erie.
Games include: Saddle Sores; Hook, Line, and Swingers; Rotating Surfboard of Death; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
News anchors challenge lumberjacks (and lumberjills) in this edition of MXC. Also, Vic gives being a lumberjack a try and Guy shows his flair for broadcast journalism.
Games include: Slippery Slope of Slanted Death; Dash to Death; Wall Bangers; Pole Riders

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: This installment of MXC features a classic battle between the Mail bags and checkered flags as The Postal Industry takes on Motor Sports. Games - Dead Letter Zone, Foul Balls, Irritable Bowl Syndrome and Log Drop.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jane's brother Trent, who's also the lead singer of local grunge band "Mystik Spiral" is offered the chance to play in the upcoming Alternapaloosa tour. Since Daria harbor's an undeniable crush on Trent, Jane offers to pay for the gas in their van and take her for their attempted tour. The fashion club, and other Lawndale High students try to go to Alternapaloosa as well.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jake's stress level is reaching critical mass, so the Morgendorffers go on a camping trip. Like that's gonna help.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale High is abuzz with homecoming preparations. The quarterback who led the team to a state championship three years ago is returning! Then he has a tragic accident, which ironically leads to sudden popularity for our Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria reluctantly gets involved in a poster contest in which she and Jane depict "Student Life At The Dawn of the Millennium"... rather negatively.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Lawndale High School goes on another field trip, this time to a local paint-ball course, with Helen & Jake as parental chaperones. The true savage nature of everybody's personality is revealed on this trip. Daria and Jane opt to sneak out toward another nearby tourist attraction that proves to be a complete rip-off.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn needs a quick passing grade in English, and ends up writing an impressive essay(by Lawndale standards, that is), but fears that it might give her a reputation as a nerd. Instead it gives her a new following as a trendy intellectual, something that Daria, a true intellectual can't stand. Since desperate times call for desperate measures, Daria is willing to consider such measures in order to persuade her sister to give up this facade.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and her family participate in a strange and frightening ritual known as a family wedding.

programme.title: Daria
programme.sub-title: That Was Then, This Is Dumb
programme.desc: Helen and Jake's former-hippie friends Willow and Coyote Yeager visit, causing the Morgendorffers to doubt their own value system. Daria and Quinn stick around to use their protest activities against them--and so Quinn can flirt with their son--until Jane invites Daria to help Trent and Jesse sell their parents' old records at a flea market.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Next Ice Age Begins at Midnight
programme.desc: Greenback threatens to use his weather control machine to create an ice age if he is not crowned king of the world.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When the United Nations inform Colonel K that aliens from another planet have announced their imminent arrival in Scotland, he sends his two top agents, Dangermouse and Penfold to meet them. Penfold describes the mechanical being they encounter as a 'clockwork mushroom'.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback has invented an 'Auto McCrikey' that can overtake other mechanical devices by remote control. In order to use it on Dangermouse's Mark 3 flying car, he orders his henchman, Stiletto Mafiosa, to lure their arch enemy into a trap. The Italian crow does this by threatening no 10 Downing street with an inflatable tank.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Agent 57 sends word to H.Q. that Greenback has build his latest hideaway on a pinnacle of rock in the middle of a volcanic crater in Northen Iceland. Furthermore, 57 has disguised himself as a flat battery, so the Baron can't start his Froghead flyer. When Dangermouse and Penfold arrive on the scene, they bump into Grigious M. Murphy, a gadget salesman who tries to interest them in a personal combat accessory kit.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K and the houses of parliament have been hit by 'green plus bombs' and have aged 25 years (at least). As Danger Mouse and Penfold close in on Baron Greenback's hideout, they get hit by one green plus and one red minus bomb each, turning Penfold into an old age hamster and Dangermouse a schoolboy detective.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has invited Dangermouse and Penfold to an old deserted funfair to meet under a flag of truce. Naturally they suspect a trap. Indeed, the villainous toad plans to fire his GB funfair finding, appointment keeping, mouse seeking, Thrissile Missile at the world's greatest detective and his not so very good assistant. However, he did not foresee his pet caterpillar Nero would get too close to the missile and gains super powers.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Straw & Acid - Straw Oboe - Ant Farmers - Rolling Pin Code - Equal Solids - Hawaiian Volcano - Swallow Upside Down - Crush Egg

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Dry Ice Fog - Napkin Suction - Crawling Cell - Computer Graph - Starch Test - Lizard Autopsy - Find Your Pulse - What Makes Rain

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Depicting Liza as the moon and Lester as the Sun, Beakman demonstrates the phases of the moon, answers meaningful questions such as "Do penguins have knees?", and explains how elevators work.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: It's microprocessor madness when Liza creates a video game to uncover the secrets of computers. Beakman dives into dentistry when Lester gets a cavity.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman's timid brother discovers that a visit to the doctor really isn't all that painful. Beakman also traces the evolution of oil from fossils.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Ben Franklin drops by the lab to help Beakman dispell some myths surrounding his inventions. Lester is shocked to learn that his recorded voice is different from the one he hears and Beakman reveals how chemicals react to their new partners.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Ants, Beakmania & Collisons--A day in the park is no picnic when a colony of ants make off with Beakman's food; Liza introduces Heimlich and his manuever; and Lester runs into trouble trying to explain collisions.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Bill Nye sheds light on solar flares, eclipses, sunspots, fusion, and solar energy, and visits an enormous solar energy farm outside Sacramento, California, to demonstrate how the sun is the source of energy for all living things on Earth.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Bill Nye's lecture is all about our brain which physically understands the words we see and hear. It controls our heart, our oxygen, our body temperature, and even our stomach. Our brain smells, sees, thinks, moves, and remembers all kinds of things all the time as long as it operates everyday.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin become unwitting accomplices in a stick-up at a gas station.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Principal Brown is concerned about a picture that Anais drew of her family. He decides the family is dysfunctional. Now Nicole has to stay home, Richard must get a job, and the boys work with Mr. Small to vent their energy.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Nicole is going shopping. Richard must work while she is gone but Gumball and Darwin show up to ask if he wants to play, but he doesn't want to. So afterwards they have a challenge to determine who is the laziest. Gumball and Darwin go to the convenience store to meet the shop owner Larry.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When a ghost named Carrie possesses Gumball's body in order to feel earthly experiences and to eat, he must learn to say "no" to her.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin's pranks on their father get out of hand.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin are ridiculed for their martial-arts outfits by their schoolmates, and their mom tries to convince them to take them off.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and his friends are invited to a high-school party hosted by Tobias's sister Rachel, but they must find dates in order to attend.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball decides to help Bobert act like a real boy. However, it soon becomes a battle for his very identity when the robot plots to take over his life.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball competes in a race in order to win Dad back.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg tells the other Titans that TV wrestling is fake and tries to teach them about real wrestling.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans are transported inside a fantasy adventure game and must find the key to return home.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven teaches the other Titans how to dance properly but then they are captured by the Dance Demon.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans reminisce about the time they threw a surprise party for Cinderblock.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans try to remember how to be superheroes in order to stop The Brain.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Santa tries to take over Halloween, it's up to the Titans and a few ghouls to stop him.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg and Starfire booby-trap the Tower for safety.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Starfire wins a goldfish at the carnival and vows to keep it alive.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Batman and Commissioner Gordon check in on various happenings around Jump City.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Santa decides to quit, the Titans pitch in to save Christmas.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: On Beast Boy's Birthday, Starfire complains because she has never celebrated a birthday on Earth. Beast Boy allows her to celebrate her birthday on the same day as his, but Beast Boy and Starlite get mad at each other after facing the struggle of sharing a birthday.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc:
Annoyed by Stimpy's stupidity, Ren goes to live off as a hermit, and goes crazy after months of being alone. He is kicked out of the hermits union for having imaginary friends.
Segment: Untamed World: Field Guide - Stimpy, reprising his role as a nature show host from Untamed World, in the jungles of Miami, Florida.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: The chums seek refuge in a model home of the future which is awash with rare conveniences.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy has a friend in his face (like a tick), then Ren ends up with the friend's friend, which starts to annoy him to no end.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren goes into Stimpy's body to find a medical explanation to why he is so stupid.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy go to the Natural History Museum to see the skeletal remains of the mammoth Stimpysaurus. Wilbur Cobb tells them about the evolution of the great beast and says that even the meat-eating Renosaurus couldn't conquer the huge bumbling idiot.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Famished, Ren & Stimpy pretend to be long lost children of two Farmers.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy are two starving peasants in a fairy-tale world, so Stimpy tries to wrangle some wheels of magic singing cheese from the village idiot by besting him in a contest of stupidity.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Renwaldo sends Stimpleton on a food mission to trade their last chigger for some cheese. Stimpy meets a village idiot and trades his chigger for the magical golden singing cheeses.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Anderson asks Beavis and Butt-head to go shopping for him at the drug store; they buy everything except what was on his list.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head finally manage to get their hands on some (non-alcoholic) beer and get "fit-shaced".

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head are recruited for an amateur game of football, the rules of which they don't understand.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After chugging Burger World's new, high-caffeine ""Volt Cola,"" Beavis transforms into Cornholio and is picked up by the Immigration Department and deported to Mexico as an illegal alien.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Panthers (Irvin & Shaunda) vs. No Names (Colleen & John)

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Whatchamacallits (Mary Ellen & Fred) vs. Airheads (Nicole & Jon)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Green Slime is Scraped from Smokers Lungs will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast really begins to think that green slime is made from the mucus from the lungs of smokers.
